summary: Corrected the include guard in src/melee/ft/chara/ftMasterHand/ftMasterHand.h from the unrelated Mars guard name to a Master Hand-specific guard. This was a small header hygiene fix affecting the fighter character headers.
what_changed: Changed the header guard macro in ftMasterHand.h from _ftmars_h_ to _ftmasterhand_h_.
Updated both the #ifndef and #define lines so the guard name matches the file and character module.
smart_moves: Kept the fix minimal: only the incorrect include guard lines were changed.
Used a guard name that directly corresponds to the header path/name, reducing risk of accidental guard collisions with other fighter headers.
decomp_lessons: When adding or copying character headers, verify that include guards are renamed from the source template or copied file.
Incorrect include guards can silently suppress inclusion of unrelated headers if two files share the same guard macro.
Header hygiene fixes are small but important in a decompilation codebase where many fighter modules have similar file structures and are often cloned or adapted.
naming_conventions: Fighter character header guards use a lowercase file-derived pattern such as _ftmasterhand_h_.
Include guard macro names should match the owning header, not the presumed source of a copied template.
follow_up_queries: Find other headers whose include guard macro does not match the file name.
Search for duplicate include guard macros across src/melee/ft/chara.
Check copied fighter headers for stale character names in guards or declarations.
